architect_review_iteration13: REJECTED — use-case lens. Require system-structure lens.
note: System-structure mapping. No implementation details. All content derived from verified Layer 0 domain artifacts.

feature_groups.name: Messaging
feature_groups.description: System pipeline structure for message dispatch and status tracking. Two parallel pipelines without coordination in sms-service. Green-ai introduces StatusProgressionGuard.

features.name: SynchronousDispatch
features.description: Direct gateway call path. Triggered by single-send (B010) or batch-activate (B004). Resolves merge fields (B017), routes to gateway via 5-branch priority (B012), writes Imported=true synchronously to SmsLog after call (B013). Broadcast prep behaviors (B001-B009: compose, activate, copy, delete) are upstream of this path.
business_rules_applied: R035 — context: sync write is ONE of TWO status write paths

features.name: AsyncDispatchPipeline
features.description: Background service that loads pending SmsLogs in three tiers (main queue + first retry + second retry) and dispatches in RoundRobin batches. Same gateway routing (B012) and sync status write (B013) as SynchronousDispatch. Kill switches: DisableMessageBackgroundService (BR019), DisableAllBroadcasts (BR020).
business_rules_applied: R035 — context: async dispatch writes same SmsLog.StatusCode as DLR callback

features.name: StatusWritePaths
features.description: TWO independent paths write to SmsLog.StatusCode and SmsLogStatuses. Path 1 (sync): SynchronousDispatch/AsyncDispatchPipeline write Imported=true after gateway call (B013). Path 2 (async DLR): gateway callbacks create SmsLogStatus records Imported=false (B014/B015), then B016 imports them in 300-chunk batches, selecting authoritative status per SmsLog. CRITICAL: paths do not coordinate (R035). SmsLogStatuses is a shared write surface: SMS + Email + Voice + Eboks all write here (R036).

features.name: StatusProgressionGuard
features.description: ARCHITECT DECISION (2026-04-13): Monotonic status state machine. Status may only move forward — never backward. A write is rejected if new_status rank <= current_status rank. Replaces the uncoordinated dual-write pattern from sms-service (R035). Terminal statuses (DELIVERED, FAILED_FINAL) cannot be overwritten by any path. Applied at the boundary of StatusWritePaths — both sync write and DLR import must pass through this guard.
business_rules_applied: R035 — root cause this guard addresses
features.architect_decision: 2026-04-13 — monotonic progression guard. NOT rowversion. Callback path cannot overwrite higher status.
writers: Messaging domain only — enforced by guard

feature_groups.name: Customer Management
feature_groups.description: Three-tier identity model. Role-based feature gating. External access configuration.

features.name: CustomerHierarchy
features.description: Customer (legal entity) → Profile (usage rights) → User (person). ProfileUserMappings link users to profiles. CustomerAccount holds billing details.

features.name: RolePermissionGating
features.description: Profile roles evaluated at broadcast create and activate time. Roles: AlwaysOwner, CanSpecifyLookup, CanSelectLookupBusinessOrPrivate, DontSendEmail, CanSelectStdReceivers, CanSendByOnlyStdReceivers, BroadcastNoReciept, HasBenchmark, HasInfoPortal. NOT enforced at DB level — application-layer gate only.

features.name: ExternalAccessManagement
features.description: API keys (CustomerApiKey, scoped: ApiSendSingle / ApiHandleRecipients / ApiHandleMessageTemplates) and FTP access (FtpUser) as separate configuration domains under Customer.
readers: Messaging API endpoints (API key auth gate)

feature_groups.name: Subscription / StandardReceiver
feature_groups.description: Customer-defined receiver lists and per-country pricing.

features.name: StandardReceiverManagement
features.description: StandardReceivers (subscriptions) per customer. Grouped via StandardReceiverGroups. Keywords for SMS opt-in. DistributionPhone for stats. ProfileStandardReceiverMappings scope per-profile access.

features.name: ReceiverPriceMatrix
features.description: Price per (CustomerStandardReceiver, CountryId). CustomerStandardReceiverSettings allows per-customer override. Stats stored at SmsGroupItem level, not per-message.

feature_groups.name: Data Import
feature_groups.description: Two-phase import pipeline with crash-safe lifecycle, size-based routing, and idempotent resume behavior.

features.name: ImportPrepare
features.description: Phase 1: upload to blob, define column mappings, run pre-validation and full row validation. Status: Queued(1)→ProcessingColumns(2)→ColumnsReady(3)→ReadyForImport(4) → or Error(-1). ConvertingFile(20) sub-state for format conversion.

features.name: ImportConfirm
features.description: Phase 2: commit validated rows. CommitStarted(107) written BEFORE data write (crash-resume marker), then ImportCommitted(7) on success. Error(-1) on failure. Post-processors run per ImportFormat after commit.

features.name: TwoPhaseImportLifecycle
features.description: System-level view of R041 state machine. Two explicit phases separated by ReadyForImport(4) gate. Phase 1 (Prepare): Queued→ColumnsReady. Phase 2 (Confirm): DataImporting(5)→DataImported(6), mediated by CommitStarted(107) crash-guard. Error(-1) is terminal failure in either phase.

features.name: InlineVsBatchExecution
features.description: Size gate (R042): DataImportFile.SizeKb < 10 → run confirm inline (same request). SizeKb >= 10 → dispatch CreateConfirmDataImportAzureTaskAsync (Azure Batch). Same confirm logic runs in both contexts. Mirrors the SmsGroup lookup routing pattern (R028/B005).

features.name: IdempotentResumeBehavior
features.description: CommitStarted(107) written atomically to DataImportFile BEFORE any data write. On restart/crash: if status = CommitStarted, resume from last committed row. If status = DataImported(6) or Error(-1): reject re-run (terminal). Prevents double-commit without distributed transaction.

feature_groups.name: Address Resolution
feature_groups.description: Per-country address resolution infrastructure. One domain, country-specific adapters. Core only sees canonical Kvhx string.

features.name: AddressLookupExecution
features.description: Runs address lookup for a broadcast via CQRS priority-queue pipeline (LookupExecutor, verified: LookupExecutor.cs:42). Commands queued, dispatched to typed processors (ILookupCommandProcessor), emit events (ILookupEvent). Runs in both web-server (inline) and Azure Batch contexts.

features.name: AddressImportCountryAdapters
features.description: Per-country import pipelines feed country-native staging tables. DK: Datafordeleren CSV → dbo.Addresses (Kvhx PK). NO: Kartverket → staging → dbo.AddressNorwegianProperties (5-tuple). SE: Lantmäteriet. FI: DEFERRED (AvoinData closed Feb 2025). Two parallel DK pipelines not yet converged.

features.name: OwnershipLookup
features.description: Resolve property owner (name, contact) from Kvhx. DK: dbo.AddressOwners + staging dbo.AddressDatafordelerOwners. NO: AddressImportNorwegianOwners + AddressImportNorwegianPeople. Change tracking: AddressOwnerChange (Kvhx rename events), AddressChange (manual corrections).

features.name: AddressResolutionFlow
features.description: System-level flow: external source → country-native staging → Kvhx string output. ALL lookups regardless of country output canonical Kvhx. Flow: ExternalSource → CountryAdapter.Import → CountryNativeStore → CountryAdapter.Resolve → Kvhx. Core domain receives only Kvhx — never country-specific structure.

features.name: CountryAdapterRouting
features.description: IAddressAdapter per country. Routing by CountryId: DK → DatafordelerenAdapter, NO → KartverketAdapter (5-tuple → Kvhx resolution), SE → LantmaterietAdapter, FI → DeferredAdapter (blocked: source closed). Adapter selection happens at infrastructure layer — core domain calls generic interface.

features.name: AddressCacheResponsibility
features.description: Each country adapter owns its cache/store with its native structure. DK cache: dbo.Addresses (Kvhx PK, string). NO cache: dbo.AddressNorwegianProperties (5-tuple key). SE cache: dbo.AddressSwedishProperties. Only Kvhx string crosses the cache boundary to core. Cache invalidation is per-country — replacing FI source does not affect DK/NO/SE.

feature_groups.name: Sales Pipeline
feature_groups.description: Prospect-to-customer conversion pipeline. Explicit gaps (verified absent behaviors) are represented as first-class features — absence is an implicit business rule.

features.name: ProspectLifecycle
features.description: Create and manage prospect entities. Contact persons, pre-configured products (ProspectProduct), pre-configured user roles (ProspectUserRoleMapping), and process tasks (ProcessTask with owner_type=Prospect). TaskOwnershipModel governs how tasks are owned and what happens at conversion.

features.name: ProspectToCustomerConversion
features.description: Two-step non-atomic conversion. Step 1 (PATCH UpdateProspectWithCustomerIdAndAddContactPersonsToCustomer): links Prospect.CustomerId, copies ContactPersons to Customer. Step 2 (PATCH CopyProspectProductsToCustomer): DESTRUCTIVE — deletes CustomerProducts then creates from ProspectProducts. Two separate API calls required — no transaction wrapping both steps.

features.name: ConversionWithoutTaskMigration
features.description: VERIFIED ABSENT (R039). Conversion code (both PATCHes) does NOT transfer ProcessTasks from Prospect to Customer. ARCHITECT DECISION (2026-04-13): LEAVE ON PROSPECT. Tasks remain as historical record on Prospect. Customer starts without inherited tasks. This is an intentional gap — not a bug to fix in green-ai without explicit business requirement.
business_rules_applied: R039 — ISOLATED UNKNOWN in sms-service. ARCHITECT DECIDED: LEAVE ON PROSPECT.
features.architect_decision: 2026-04-13 — tasks stay on Prospect. No reassignment to Customer at conversion.

features.name: ConversionWithoutRoleActivation
features.description: VERIFIED ABSENT (R040). ProspectUserRoleMappings are NOT consumed at conversion. No code reads them to activate roles on the new Customer. ARCHITECT DECISION (2026-04-13): implicit business rule via absence. Green-ai must NOT auto-activate roles at conversion unless a new explicit business requirement is added.
business_rules_applied: R040 — ISOLATED UNKNOWN in sms-service. Intentional absence.
features.architect_decision: 2026-04-13 — role pre-config is NOT applied at conversion. Absence is the business rule.

features.name: TaskOwnershipModel
features.description: ProcessTask is polymorphic: owned by Prospect OR Customer. Ownership via owner_type + owner_id (no DB referential integrity). ProcessTaskTemplate determines allowed owner_type. ARCHITECT DECISION (iteration 10): enforced in application layer — NOT polymorphic FK in DB. At conversion: tasks remain on Prospect (see ConversionWithoutTaskMigration).
features.architect_decision: app-layer enforcement. owner_type determines valid owner_id lookup. Template constrains owner_type.

system_concerns.name: DualPipelineRaceCondition
system_concerns.description: SynchronousDispatch (B013) and StatusWritePaths DLR import (B016) both write SmsLog.StatusCode without coordination. Last writer wins. In sms-service: unresolved. In green-ai: resolved by StatusProgressionGuard (FG001-S04).
system_concerns.green_ai_resolution: StatusProgressionGuard — monotonic state machine. Only forward transitions allowed.

system_concerns.name: CrossDomainWriteSurface_SmsLogStatuses
system_concerns.description: dbo.SmsLogStatuses is written by SMS, Email, Voice, and Eboks status writers. No domain boundary enforcement at write level in sms-service. Messaging is NOT an isolated domain at this surface.
system_concerns.green_ai_resolution: Messaging domain owns SmsLogStatuses. Email/Voice/Eboks write via IDeliveryStatusWriter contract — not directly to table. StatusProgressionGuard applies to all writers.

system_concerns.name: PartialLifecycle_ProspectConversion
system_concerns.description: Two-step non-atomic conversion (R038). Tasks NOT migrated (R039). Roles NOT activated (R040). The Prospect→Customer lifecycle is intentionally partial — three things that COULD happen do NOT happen. Absence is the business rule.
system_concerns.green_ai_resolution: Represent as explicit features (ConversionWithoutTaskMigration, ConversionWithoutRoleActivation). Do not add migration logic without new business requirement.

system_concerns.name: ImportIdempotency
system_concerns.description: DataImport two-phase commit uses CommitStarted(107) as crash-resume marker. Terminal states (DataImported=6, Error=-1) prevent re-run. Without this guard: double-commit is possible on crash+restart.
system_concerns.green_ai_resolution: IdempotentResumeBehavior (FG004-S03) captures this explicitly. CommitStarted state must be preserved in green-ai import pipeline.

system_concerns.name: AddressAdapterIsolation
system_concerns.description: Four national address systems with incompatible schemas. FI source closed Feb 2025. Two parallel DK pipelines not converged. If adapter boundaries are not maintained, country-specific logic bleeds into core domain.
system_concerns.green_ai_resolution: CountryAdapterRouting + AddressCacheResponsibility (FG005-S02/S03) enforce the boundary. Core domain never receives country-native data structures.

status_progression_guard.decision: Monotonic status state machine (NOT rowversion). Status rank only increases. Callback path cannot overwrite a higher-ranked status. Terminal statuses (DELIVERED, FAILED_FINAL) are immutable.
status_progression_guard.rejected: rowversion / optimistic concurrency — too coarse, doesn't prevent logical regression
status_progression_guard.green_ai_requirement: StatusProgressionGuard applied at all SmsLog status write points. Status ranks must be defined in domain model before implementation.

smslogstatuses_ownership.decision: Messaging domain owns SmsLogStatuses. Email, Voice, Eboks are consumers — they write via IDeliveryStatusWriter contract, not directly. System concern SC002.
smslogstatuses_ownership.green_ai_requirement: IDeliveryStatusWriter interface defined in Messaging domain. Other channel domains depend on Messaging for status writing.

address_country_adapters.decision: One Address domain, per-country infrastructure adapters. Core receives only Kvhx. AddressCacheResponsibility per country.

process_task_conversion.decision: LEAVE ON PROSPECT. Tasks remain as historical record. Customer starts clean. No reassignment logic at conversion.
process_task_conversion.rationale: Code does not do it today. Implicit rule via absence. Avoids hidden data mutation without explicit business requirement.
process_task_conversion.green_ai_requirement: ConversionWithoutTaskMigration (FG006-G01) is a hard constraint. Do not add task migration without new explicit requirement.

last_minute_lookup_threshold.rationale: Buffer for lookup + queue latency. Matches typical Azure Batch job scheduling window.
last_minute_lookup_threshold.green_ai_requirement: ScheduledLookupTrigger checks: ACTIVATED broadcasts with LastMinuteLookup=true AND DateDelayToUtc within 5 minutes. Configurable via AppSetting.

cross_domain_dependency_matrix.Messaging → Address Resolution: DISPATCH: activation dispatches lookup. Receives Kvhx back as SmsLog records.
cross_domain_dependency_matrix.Messaging → Customer: READ: profile roles at create + activate. WRITE: receipt emails post-send.
cross_domain_dependency_matrix.Messaging → StandardReceiver: READ: default SmsGroupItems at broadcast create.
cross_domain_dependency_matrix.Address Resolution → Messaging: OUTPUT: resolved Kvhx in SmsLog records.
cross_domain_dependency_matrix.Customer ← Sales Pipeline: WRITE: conversion writes CustomerId to Prospect; creates ContactPerson + CustomerProduct on Customer.
cross_domain_dependency_matrix.Messaging ← DataImport: INDIRECT: large imports feed Address domain which feeds Messaging lookup pipeline.
cross_domain_dependency_matrix.SmsLogStatuses ← all channels (SC002): CRITICAL: SMS + Email + Voice + Eboks. Messaging owns table. Others write via IDeliveryStatusWriter contract (green-ai requirement).
cross_domain_dependency_matrix.StatusProgressionGuard → all status writ: CROSS-CUTTING CONCERN: all write paths to SmsLog.StatusCode pass through monotonic guard.
